skill.name: operating-production-services
skill.description: SRE guidance for operating production services with SLOs, error budgets, incident postmortems, on-call practices, and Prometheus alerting patterns.
skill.summary: Plan and improve production reliability with SLOs, error budgets, postmortems, and alerting guidance.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: AI review found that the static external command, weak crypto, and reconnaissance detections are false positives in documentation examples. The skill contains Markdown guidance, PromQL/YAML snippets, and a local verifier script with no network access, secret collection, prompt injection, or executable shell behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated Markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby shell execution. These locations are documentation fences or PromQL/YAML examples, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences or embedded observability examples. No Ruby code, shell invocation, user input, or command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose and Templates
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive text, alert annotations, and SLO template fields. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary Markdown, YAML labels, and prose at the flagged lines. There is no cryptographic operation to misuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dashboard Text Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections point to an ASCII dashboard mockup. It displays example SLO status text and does not collect host, network, user, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are static dashboard illustration text. They contain no commands, probes, filesystem traversal, or runtime data collection.

content.value_statement: Production teams need clear reliability targets and repeatable incident practices. This skill provides SRE templates for SLOs, error budgets, alerting, and postmortems.
actual_capabilities: Explains how SLAs, SLOs, and SLIs relate to production reliability.
Provides common availability and latency SLI formulas for Prometheus metrics.
Defines error budget policies for normal work, risky changes, and freezes.
Supplies standard and quick postmortem templates for incidents.
Provides multi-window burn rate alert examples and SLO dashboard guidance.
Includes a simple verifier script for checking required skill files and sections.
limitations: It does not connect to monitoring systems or deploy alert rules automatically.
Prometheus examples require adaptation to each service metric schema.
It is not intended for initial service scaffolding or feature development.
It provides operational templates, not legal SLA advice.

use_cases.title: Define Service Reliability Targets
use_cases.description: Create practical SLOs, SLIs, and error budget policies for an existing production service.

use_cases.title: Run Incident Reviews
use_cases.description: Draft a blameless postmortem with timeline, impact, root cause analysis, and action items.

use_cases.title: Improve Alert Quality
use_cases.description: Replace noisy single-window alerts with burn rate alerts tied to user-visible reliability goals.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Initial SLOs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to suggest availability and latency SLOs for an existing API. Include SLIs, target values, and error budget meaning.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Error Budget Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft an error budget policy for a 99.9 percent monthly availability SLO. Include actions for healthy, low, and exhausted budgets.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Postmortem
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to write a blameless postmortem for a SEV2 outage. Include summary, UTC timeline, impact, 5 Whys, and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Design Burn Rate Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design multi-window burn rate alerts for API availability. Include fast burn, slow burn, severity, and dashboard fields.

output_examples.input: Define reliability targets for a payments API.
output_examples.output: A concise SLO plan with availability and latency SLIs, monthly targets, error budget size, and policy actions.

output_examples.input: Summarize a customer-facing outage after recovery.
output_examples.output: A blameless postmortem outline with executive summary, timeline, impact, root cause analysis, and tracked follow-up actions.
A burn rate alerting plan that separates urgent budget burn from slower reliability degradation.

best_practices: Start with user-visible availability and latency before internal technical metrics.
Tie alert severity to error budget burn rate and customer impact.
Assign every postmortem action item an owner, due date, and tracking ticket.
anti_patterns: Do not target 100 percent reliability unless the business cost justifies it.
Do not alert directly on raw SLIs without burn rate context.
Do not write postmortems that blame individuals instead of improving systems.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps teams define SLOs, manage error budgets, improve alerts, and write incident postmortems.

faq.question: Does it deploy Prometheus rules?
faq.answer: No. It provides example rules and patterns that engineers must adapt and deploy themselves.

faq.question: Can it create legal SLAs?
faq.answer: No. It explains the relationship between SLAs and SLOs, but legal agreements need qualified review.

faq.question: Is it useful after a production incident?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes standard and quick postmortem structures for incident learning and follow-up.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill access secrets or external systems?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain documentation and a local structure verification script.
